blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: Walk me through the locked room. Because right now that's the wall.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: It's not a wall. It's a misdirection. A very good one — good enough that it almost closed this case before it opened. Here's what the locked room actually tells you.
He leaned forward slightly. Not for emphasis — it was just how he thought. Closer to the problem.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: A locked door eliminates one category of murder. Physical presence in the room at the time of death. That's all it eliminates. It says nothing about what happened to Marcus in the hours before he walked upstairs. If someone gave him something — something already working its way through his system — he could lock that door himself, lie down, and die completely alone. The locked room doesn't protect whoever did this. It's exactly what they wanted you to focus on.
Davenport didn't write anything. He was looking at Julian the way experienced men look at something they'd almost missed.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Months of it. Someone chose that house, that weekend, those people. Everything about this was decided long before any of them packed a bag.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: That's a significant claim without physical evidence.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: The tox screen will give you your physical evidence. What I'm giving you now is the shape of what you're looking at so you know what to look for.
A beat. Davenport picked up his pen.
